title: evallaria verticillata
intro: Evallaria verticillata, also known as the Cobra Lily, is a rare and unusual plant species native to the tropical rainforests of Sri Lanka. It is characterized by its unique, hood-like spathe that opens to reveal a long, tubular spur, earning it the name Cobra Lily due to its resemblance to a cobra's hood. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving digestive issues. Evallaria verticillata is often used in herbal preparations, including tinctures and infusions, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine.
